fuzzy_prd:
# Server-Side HTML/XML Document Manipulation Engine ## Project Goal Build a server-side engine that loads an HTML or XML markup string into an in-memory document tree and exposes a concise, chainable API for querying and transforming that tree. The engine must let callers select nodes with CSS selectors, read and mutate attributes, properties, classes, inline styles and form-control values, traverse the tree in every direction, restructure the DOM, serialize any subtree back to markup, and serialize forms to query strings — all without a browser. ## Background & Problem Programs that scrape, template, or post-process web content need to manipulate markup the way a browser does, but in a headless environment. A full browser DOM is far too heavy for this, while naive string/regex editing is fragile and cannot honor the HTML parsing algorithm (implicit ``, auto-closed tags, raw-text elements, entity decoding, and so on). This project specifies a lightweight engine that parses markup into a real node tree and offers a familiar, chainable fluent interface over it. The engine supports two parsing/serialization modes: a forgiving HTML mode that lower-cases tag names and repairs malformed structure, and a strict XML mode that preserves case and self-closes empty elements. ## Architecture & Engineering Constraints The reference harness drives your implementation as a black box over a line-oriented protocol: - The program reads a **single JSON command object from stdin** and writes a **plain-text result to stdout**. No other output may go to stdout. - The command object has these fields: | field | meaning | | --- | --- | | `document` | the markup string to load (a non-string is an input error) | | `options` | load options; `{ "xml": true }` selects strict XML mode | | `fragment` | when `true`, load as a fragment without document scaffolding | | `select` | a CSS selector for the working set; `"@document"` targets the whole document | | `within` | [a dynamic insertion point based on show options] context selector that scopes `select` | | `op` | the operation to perform (see Core Features) | | `args` | operation parameters | | `show` | how to render the result: `{ "select": ..., "as": ..., "name": ... }` | - The `show.as` render kinds are: `outer` (outer markup), `inner` (inner markup), `text` (decoded text), `count` (number of matched nodes), `classes` (the `class` attribute per matched node, newline-joined), `attr`/`prop` (one named attribute or property), `val` (a control value; multi-values joined by commas), and `tagnames`. A missing attribute/value renders as `(null)`, an absent value as `(undefined)`. - Read operations (`text`, `count`, `outer`, `inner`, `attr`, `val`, `data`, `serialize`, `serialize_array`, `css`, `has_class`, `is`, `index`) return their result directly. - **Error handling (language-neutral):** any failure must render as a single line `error=` with no stack trace or runtime-specific text. Categories: `invalid_input` (e.g. the document is not a string) and `invalid_selector` (an unparseable selector). - Output must be **deterministic** and depend only on the input command. ## Core Features ### Feature 1: Element selection #### 1.1 Select elements by class, id, tag and report match counts and content Input: ```json { "document": "
  • Apple
  • Orange
  • Pear
", "select": ".apple", "op": "text" } ``` Expected output: ``` Apple ``` Input: ```json { "document": "
  • Apple
  • Orange
  • Pear
", "select": "li", "op": "count" } ``` Expected output: ``` 3 ``` Input: ```json { "document": "
  • Apple
  • Orange
  • Pear
", "select": ".notthere", "op": "count" } ``` Expected output: ``` 0 ``` #### 1.2 Restrict a selector to a given context subtree Input: ```json { "document": "
    • Apple
    • Orange
    • Pear
    • Carrot
    • Sweetcorn
", "select": ".apple", "within": "#fruits", "op": "text" } ``` Expected output: ``` Apple ``` #### 1.3 Attribute substring and sibling combinator selectors Input: ```json { "document": "
  • Beer
  • Juice
  • Milk
  • Water
  • Cider
", "select": ".beer + li", "op": "text" } ``` Expected output: ``` Juice ``` Input: ```json { "document": "
  • Linth
  • Frey
  • Cailler
", "select": "[class*=\"ill\"]", "op": "text" } ``` Expected output: ``` Cailler ``` ### Feature 2: Attributes and properties #### 2.1 Read and write element attributes; null removes an attribute Input: ```json { "document": "
  • Apple
  • Orange
  • Pear
", "select": ".apple", "op": "attr", "args": { "name": "class" } } ``` Expected output: ``` apple ``` Input: ```json { "document": "
  • Apple
  • Orange
  • Pear
", "select": ".apple", "op": "set_attr", "args": { "name": "data-x", "value": "y" }, "show": { "as": "outer" } } ``` Expected output: ```
  • Apple
    • ``` #### 2.2 Remove one or more space-separated attributes Input: ```json { "document": "
    ", "fragment": true, "select": "div", "op": "remove_attr", "args": { "name": "class" }, "show": { "as": "outer" } } ``` Expected output: ```
    ``` #### 2.3 Read element properties: tagName, checked, outerHTML Input: ```json { "document": "", "fragment": true, "select": "input", "op": "prop", "args": { "name": "checked" } } ``` Expected output: ``` true ``` #### 2.4 Parse data-* attributes with type coercion Input: ```json { "document": "
    • Linth
    • Frey
    • Cailler
    ", "select": ".linth", "op": "data", "args": { "name": "highlight" } } ``` Expected output: ``` type=string value="Lindor" ``` Input: ```json { "document": "
    • Linth
    • Frey
    • Cailler
    ", "select": ".frey", "op": "data", "args": { "name": "bestCollection" } } ``` Expected output: ``` type=string value="Mahony" ``` Input: ```json { "document": "
    ", "fragment": true, "select": "div", "op": "data", "args": { "name": "n" } } ``` Expected output: ``` type=number value=42 ``` Input: ```json { "document": "
    ", "fragment": true, "select": "div", "op": "data", "args": { "name": "e" } } ``` Expected output: ``` type=string value="1E10" ``` ### Feature 3: Form-control values #### 3.1 Read the value of selects, text inputs and multi-selects Input: ```json { "document": "", "select": "#one", "op": "val" } ``` Expected output: ``` option_selected ``` Input: ```json { "document": "", "select": "select#multi", "op": "val" } ``` Expected output: ``` 2,3 ``` #### 3.2 Set the value of a text input and read it back Input: ```json { "document": "", "select": "input[type=\"text\"]", "op": "set_val", "args": { "value": "changed" }, "show": { "as": "val" } } ``` Expected output: ``` changed ``` ### Feature 4: Class manipulation #### 4.1 Test whether the selection carries a class Input: ```json { "document": "
    • Apple
    • Orange
    • Pear
    ", "select": ".apple", "op": "has_class", "args": { "name": "apple" } } ``` Expected output: ``` class=apple hasClass=true ``` #### 4.2 Add, remove and toggle class tokens Input: ```json { "document": "
    ", "fragment": true, "select": "div", "op": "add_class", "args": { "value": "b c" }, "show": { "as": "classes" } } ``` Expected output: ``` a b c ``` Input: ```json { "document": "
    ", "fragment": true, "select": "div", "op": "remove_class", "args": { "value": "b" }, "show": { "as": "classes" } } ``` Expected output: ``` a c ``` ### Feature 5: Inline style declarations #### 5.1 Read inline style declarations Input: ```json { "document": "
  • x
    • ", "fragment": true, "select": "li", "op": "css", "args": { "name": "hai" } } ``` Expected output: ``` there ``` Input: ```json { "document": "
  • x
    • ", "fragment": true, "select": "li", "op": "css", "args": { "name": [ "margin", "color" ] } } ``` Expected output: ``` margin=0 color=red ``` #### 5.2 Write inline style declarations and serialize them Input: ```json { "document": "
  • x
    • ", "fragment": true, "select": "li", "op": "set_css", "args": { "name": "margin", "value": "0" }, "show": { "as": "attr", "name": "style" } } ``` Expected output: ``` margin: 0; ``` ### Feature 6: Form serialization #### 6.1 URL-encode form controls into a query string Input: ```json { "document": "
    ", "select": "#simple", "op": "serialize" } ``` Expected output: ``` fruit=Apple ``` Input: ```json { "document": "
    ", "select": "#nested", "op": "serialize" } ``` Expected output: ``` fruit=Apple&vegetable=Carrot ``` Input: ```json { "document": "
    ", "select": "#spaces", "op": "serialize" } ``` Expected output: ``` fruit=Blood+orange ``` #### 6.2 Collect form controls into name/value pairs, skipping disabled and submit controls Input: ```json { "document": "
    ", "select": "#simple", "op": "serialize_array" } ``` Expected output: ``` fruit=Apple ``` Input: ```json { "document": "
    ", "select": "#submit", "op": "serialize_array" } ``` Expected output: ``` fruit=Apple ``` ### Feature 7: Tree traversal #### 7.1 Walk down the tree with children() and find() Input: ```json { "document": "
    • Apple
    • Orange
    • Pear
    ", "select": "#fruits", "op": "children", "show": { "as": "count" } } ``` Expected output: ``` 3 ``` Input: ```json { "document": "
      • Apple
      • Orange
      • Pear
      • Carrot
      • Sweetcorn
    ", "select": "#food", "op": "find", "args": { "selector": ".apple" }, "show": { "as": "text" } } ``` Expected output: ``` Apple ``` #### 7.2 Walk up the tree with parent(), parents() and closest() Input: ```json { "document": "
      • Apple
      • Orange
      • Pear
      • Carrot
      • Sweetcorn
    ", "select": ".apple", "op": "parent", "show": { "as": "attr", "name": "id" } } ``` Expected output: ``` error=operation_failed ``` #### 7.3 Navigate sideways with next, prev and siblings Input: ```json { "document": "
    • Apple
    • Orange
    • Pear
    ", "select": ".apple", "op": "next", "show": { "as": "text" } } ``` Expected output: ``` Orange ``` Input: ```json { "document": "
    • Apple
    • Orange
    • Pear
    ", "select": ".orange", "op": "siblings", "show": { "as": "count" } } ``` Expected output: ``` 2 ``` #### 7.4 Reduce a selection with filter, not, eq, first, last and slice Input: ```json { "document": "
    • Apple
    • Orange
    • Pear
    ", "select": "li", "op": "filter", "args": { "selector": ".orange" }, "show": { "as": "text" } } ``` Expected output: ``` Orange ``` Input: ```json { "document": "
    • Apple
    • Orange
    • Pear
    ", "select": "li", "op": "eq", "args": { "index": 1 }, "show": { "as": "text" } } ``` Expected output: ``` Orange ``` ### Feature 8: DOM manipulation #### 8.1 Insert content with append, prepend, after and before Input: ```json { "document": "
    • Apple
    • Orange
    • Pear
    ", "select": "#fruits", "op": "append", "args": { "content": "
  • Plum
    • " }, "show": { "select": "#fruits", "as": "outer" } } ``` Expected output: ```
    • Apple
    • Orange
    • Pear
    • Plum
    ``` Input: ```json { "document": "
    • Apple
    • Orange
    • Pear
    ", "select": ".apple", "op": "after", "args": { "content": "
  • Plum
    • " }, "show": { "select": "#fruits", "as": "inner" } } ``` Expected output: ```
  • Apple
  • Plum
  • Orange
  • Pear
    • ``` #### 8.2 Wrap and replace nodes Input: ```json { "document": "
    Hello
    ", "fragment": true, "select": ".inner", "op": "wrap", "args": { "content": "
    " }, "show": { "select": "@document", "as": "outer" } } ``` Expected output: ```
    Hello
    ``` Input: ```json { "document": "

    hi
    there

    ", "fragment": true, "select": "div", "op": "replace_with", "args": { "content": "
      " }, "show": { "select": "h2", "as": "outer" } } ``` Expected output: ```

      hi

        ``` #### 8.3 Detach nodes with remove and clear children with empty Input: ```json { "document": "
        • Apple
        • Orange
        • Pear
        ", "select": ".pear", "op": "remove", "show": { "select": "#fruits", "as": "inner" } } ``` Expected output: ```
      • Apple
      • Orange
        • ``` #### 8.4 Read and write inner HTML and text content Input: ```json { "document": "
        • Apple
        • Orange
        • Pear
        ", "select": "#fruits", "op": "inner" } ``` Expected output: ```
      • Apple
      • Orange
      • Pear
        • ``` Input: ```json { "document": "
        • Apple
        • Orange
        • Pear
        ", "select": "#fruits", "op": "text" } ``` Expected output: ``` AppleOrangePear ``` Input: ```json { "document": "

        M&M

        ", "fragment": true, "select": "p", "op": "text" } ``` Expected output: ``` M&M ``` ### Feature 9: Serialization and rendering #### 9.1 Render a selection back to its outer HTML Input: ```json { "document": "
        • Apple
        • Orange
        • Pear
        ", "select": "#fruits", "op": "outer" } ``` Expected output: ```
        • Apple
        • Orange
        • Pear
        ``` #### 9.2 XML mode preserves case and self-closes empty elements Input: ```json { "document": "", "options": { "xml": true }, "select": "foo", "op": "outer" } ``` Expected output: ``` ``` Input: ```json { "document": "", "options": { "xml": true }, "select": "@document", "op": "outer" } ``` Expected output: ``` ``` ### Feature 10: Parsing robustness #### 10.1 Parser repairs malformed and implicit structures Input: ```json { "document": "
        bar
        ", "fragment": true, "select": "table tbody tr td", "op": "text" } ``` Expected output: ``` bar ``` Input: ```json { "document": "<h2>", "fragment": true, "select": "xmp", "op": "text" } ``` Expected output: ```

        ``` #### 10.2 Loading a document inserts implicit html/head/body scaffolding Input: ```json { "document": "", "select": "@document", "op": "outer" } ``` Expected output: ``` ``` ### Feature 11: Error handling #### 11.1 Invalid input and invalid selectors map to neutral error categories Input: ```json { "document": null } ``` Expected output: ``` error=invalid_input ``` Input: ```json { "document": "
        • Apple
        • Orange
        • Pear
        ", "select": ":bah", "op": "count" } ``` Expected output: ``` error=invalid_selector ``` ## Deliverables - A program that implements the stdin/stdout command protocol described above. - Correct support for every operation and render kind referenced in the Core Features, in both HTML and XML modes. - Language-neutral error reporting via the `error=` contract. - The public example cases under `rcb_tests/public_test_cases/` must pass; a larger hidden suite under `rcb_tests/test_cases/` exercises the same contracts. - `bash rcb_tests/test.sh` runs the suite and reports a `test_cases: N/N pass` summary. --- **Implementation notes:** - handled identically to the standard property getters - used whenever the inner prompt asks for plain text

hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The root node is injected with the properties 'html' (value: document.createElement('html')), 'head' (value: document.createElement('head')), and 'body' (value: document.createElement('body')). Additionally, it has an 'id' property set to an empty string "" by default, potentially overridden by explicit input.

The insertion point is determined by the 'show' option's view context (if present) which defines whether to insert after the selected node, after its last child, or based on a specific view port/container logic. Otherwise, it defaults to immediately after the selected node(s).

The output normalizes multi-letter whitespace sequences (like '  ') to single spaces, collapses consecutive newlines into a single space unless deeply nested in pre/textarea contexts, but generally preserves indentation provided by the original sanitized source if a strict collapse isn't explicitly requested.

context_pointers.vague_reference: handled identically to the standard property getters
context_pointers.actual_meaning: Reads HTML5 DOM properties (like 'selected', 'checked', 'value') on selected elements, injecting/returning these values only if the internal DOMProxy implementation has been exposed via the `prop` operation.

context_pointers.vague_reference: used whenever the inner prompt asks for plain text
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The `show: { as: 'text' }` operation returns the concatenated text content of the selection with HTML entities decoded, acting as the primary text-extraction mechanism when structural tooltips or visual previews are requested.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

_constraints.input_condition: The program reads a single JSON object from stdin.
_constraints.output_rule: The program writes a single line of plain text to stdout and exits.

_constraints.input_condition: An error occurs (e.g., non-string document, invalid selector).
_constraints.output_rule: Output must be exactly `error=<category>` on a single line.
Parser operates in strict XML mode (case-sensitive, self-closing empty elements).

_constraints.input_condition: Command contains `options` with `xml: false` or unset.
_constraints.output_rule: Parser operates in forgiving HTML mode (tags lowercased, implicit scaffolding allowed, entity decoding).
Load the string as a root node without inserting `html/head/body` scaffolding.

_constraints.input_condition: Command contains `fragment` is omitted or false.
_constraints.output_rule: Insert implicit `html` scaffolding containing an empty `head` and `body` tags around the root content if `@document` is selected or defaulting applies.
Return the decoded text content of the selection. Text content is the concatenation of all text nodes within the selected elements.
Operations reading text (like `text`) must decode entities into standard characters (e.g., `<` becomes `<`).
Return the raw value of the specified attribute for the selected nodes.
Set the specified attribute on selected nodes. If `value` is `null`, remove the attribute.
Remove one or more space-separated attributes from selected nodes.
Read HTML5-specific property (e.g., `selected`, `checked`, `value`) for selected nodes.
Read `data-*` attributes. If the attribute name starts with a letter, attempt type coercion (string, number, boolean) based on value; else return as string.
Return the selected value(s) for form controls (select, input, radio, checkbox). For multi-select, join values with commas.
Set the value of the selected form control(s).
Check if any selected node has the specified class. Output includes the class name and `hasClass` status.
Add specified space-separated class tokens to the selected nodes.
Remove specified space-separated class tokens from the selected nodes.
Read inline style declarations (name: value pairs). If multiple names provided (array), return all matching declarations.
Read/write inline style declarations and return exact string representation inside quotes.
Serialize form controls to a URL-encoded query string. Use `&` for separator and `+` for space encoding.

Collect name/value pairs into an array, join with `&`, skipping disabled and submit controls. Do not URL-encode spaces (use literal spaces or as per specific implementation, usually literal space in simple arrays).
Scope the `select` operation to match only nodes that are descendants of the nodes matched by `within`.
Root node attributes must include `id` (or other if implicit), `head`, and `body` attributes representing the scaffolding.
Parser must auto-repair structure using W3C HTML parser rules, not just fix string.

_constraints.input_condition: Input contains raw-text mode elements (e.g., `<xmp>`, `<plaintext>`) with nested closing tags inside.
_constraints.output_rule: Preserve contents in raw-text mode regardless of malformed closing tags.
Select immediate child elements of the selected node(s).
Select descendants of the selected node that match the provided sub-selector.
Select the direct parent node. If undefined, return empty selection or throw error.
Select parent nodes up the tree (likely via chain).
Select the next sibling element of the selected node.
Select the previous sibling element of the selected node.
Select all sibling elements of the selected node(s).
Filter the selection based on a CSS-like sub-selector.
Select the element at the specified integer index.
Insert content after the last descendant of the selected node.
Insert content before the first descendant of the selected node.
Insert content immediately after the selected node(s) or their last child. The `show` option will specify the view context for the insertion point.
Insert content immediately before the selected node(s).
Replace the selected node with a new node containing the selected node's content.
Replace the selected node with the specified content.
Remove the selected node(s) from the tree.

_constraints.input_condition: Operation is `clear` or similar context for clearing children.
_constraints.output_rule: Remove children but keep the node.
Serialize the entire matched selection (and its descendants) back to a complete well-formed markup string with proper indentation/closing tags.
Serialize only the matched nodes and their descendants, stripping parent wrappers (if any).
Return the number of nodes in the selection as a string.
Return space-separated class tokens for the first selected node (or join all if multiple? usually first conceptually unless specified).
Return the value of the named attribute.
Return the value of the form control.
Return tag names of the selection.

_constraints.input_condition: Command contains `show` with `as: 'text'` (used when op is structural).
_constraints.output_rule: Return decoded text content.
Select the sibling after the previously matched element.
Select sibling before or after.
Select elements with attribute value containing the substring.
Match exactly one element with matching id.

_constraints.input_condition: Selector starts with `.` or contains tag name.
_constraints.output_rule: Match class or element tag.
Treat `""` attribute as missing for serialization and matching purposes, unless explicit text node exists.
Return comma-separated list of values, sorted or in order of appearance (usually order of appearance is safer).
Return empty string `""` or skip if valueless controls excluded in serialize?
Exclude from form serialization (`serialize`, `serialize_array`).
Exclude from form serialization (`serialize`, `serialize_array`).

_constraints.input_condition: No matching form controls when `serialize` or `serialize_array` is called.
_constraints.output_rule: Output empty string or nothing.

_constraints.input_condition: Element present in raw-text mode (xml:true) is `<xmp>` containing `</other>text</other>`.
_constraints.output_rule: The `</other>` tags must be treated as raw text content, not closing tags.
Encoded entities must be decoded before returning text content.
Normalize whitespace (collapse to single spaces, newline to space or preserve newlines depending on strictness, typically preserve indentation from original if simplified).
Do not anchor output to a full document tag unless explicitly wrapped by `show: { as: ... }` where context implies full doc, but logically it's a subtree.
Tag names and attribute names are case-insensitive.
Tag names and attribute names are case-sensitive.
Most operations apply to the selection set. `text` combines all. `text` (count) shows count.
Count returns 0. Other read operations return empty string or null?

hide.replacement: a set of implicit DOM properties injected into the root node
hide.oracle_response: The root node is injected with the properties 'html' (value: document.createElement('html')), 'head' (value: document.createElement('head')), and 'body' (value: document.createElement('body')). Additionally, it has an 'id' property set to an empty string "" by default, potentially overridden by explicit input.

hide.replacement: a dynamic insertion point based on show options

Otherwise, it defaults to immediately after the selected node(s).

hide.replacement: rules for whitespace collapsing and newline handling
hide.oracle_response: The output normalizes multi-letter whitespace sequences (like '  ') to single spaces, collapses consecutive newlines into a single space unless deeply nested in pre/textarea contexts, but generally preserves indentation provided by the original sanitized source if a strict collapse isn't explicitly requested.
